response.justification: The candidate is a highly experienced polyglot engineer (15+ years) with strong software craftsmanship, but his profile centers on web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) rather than the hands-on modern C++ this role requires. His C++ exposure is dated and brief (National Instruments 2014–2015, a thesis project), and there is no evidence of MQTT, message brokers in C++, IEC 62443/SDL practices, or industrial automation domain experience. Systems-level strengths in low-latency, concurrent services (DLang at Dunnhumby) are adjacent but do not offset the core skills gap for a senior C++ role with security responsibilities.
strengths: 15+ years of broad engineering experience with strong testing/TDD and craftsmanship culture
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work with async I/O, caching, and concurrency (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Mentorship and knowledge-sharing track record through workshops, courses, and open-source projects
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and ~10 years old; recent work is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang
No industrial cybersecurity, SDL, or IEC 62443 exposure; no MQTT or industrial protocol experience
No industrial automation or IoT domain background aligned with the manufacturing context

key_factors.factor: No industrial security (IEC 62443/SDL) or MQTT experience
Strong concurrent/low-latency systems and mentoring background
